body:
In the following paragraphs and in regulations 9 to 12 and 14—
in the case of a single claim (but not a joint claim), the person making the claim is referred to as the “claimant”; and
in the case of a joint claim, the members of the ... couple making the claim are referred to as the “joint claimants”.
The maximum rate at which a claimant or joint claimants may be entitled to child tax credit shall be the aggregate of—
the family element of child tax credit if the claimant is, or either or both the joint claimants are, responsible for a child or qualifying young person who was born before 6thApril 2017, and
an individual element of child tax credit, in respect of each child or qualifying young person for whom—
the claimant, or
either or both of the joint claimants,
as the case may be, is or are responsible, but subject to paragraph (2A); and.
a disability element of child tax credit in the case of each child or qualifying young person who is disabled or severely disabled.
Where the claimant, or either or both of the joint claimants, is or are responsible for a child or qualifying young person born on or after 6th April 2017 (“A”), the maximum rate referred to in paragraph (2) shall not include an individual element of child tax credit in respect of A unless—
the claimant is, or the joint claimants are, claiming the individual element of child tax credit for no more than one other child or qualifying young person; or
an exception applies in relation to A in accordance with regulation 9.
The family element of child tax credit is £545.
The individual element of child tax credit for any child or qualifying young person referred to in paragraph (2)(b) above—
. . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . .
. . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . .
in the case of a child, is £2,845;
. . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . .
. . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . .
in the case of a qualifying young person, is £2,845.
The disability element of child tax credit—
where the child or qualifying young person is disabled, is £3,435;
where the child or qualifying young person is severely disabled, is £4,825.
heading: Regulation 7
